title: UK inflation: What is the rate and why are prices still rising?
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: UK inflation rose to 3.3% in March 2025, up from 3% in January and February, driven by global energy costs. The Bank of England aims for 2% inflation, but geopo
excerpt: UK inflation rose to 3.3% in March 2025, up from 3% in January and February, driven by global energy costs. The Bank of England aims for 2% inflation, but geopolitical tensions are delaying rate cuts. Core inflation was 3.1% in March 2025.

content.introduction: UK inflation rose to 3.3% in March 2025, up from 3% in January and February, driven by global energy costs. The Bank of England aims for 2% inflation, but geopolitical tensions are delaying rate cuts. Core inflation was 3.1% in March 2025.
body: # Why are UK prices rising more quickly? **Prices in the UK rose by 3.3% in the year to March, up from the 3% recorded in January and February, and above the Bank of England's 2% target.**. These were the first official inflation figures since the start of the US-Israel war with Iran, which has put up energy and fuel costs around the world. The Bank moves interest rates up and down to try to keep inflation on track. Six cuts since August 2024 had brought rates down to 3.75%, but the war is expected to delay any further falls, and the next move could be up. ## What is inflation? Inflation is the increase in the price of something over time. ## How is the UK's inflation rate measured? ## What is happening to UK inflation? Core CPI was 3.1% in the 12 months to March, down from 3.2% in February.

[## How high could UK petrol and diesel prices go?](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/c20zgjzz0e4o). The [most recent cut in December 2025](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cj01v7z73q1o) reflected concerns over rising unemployment and weak economic growth. The vote was equally tight in February, when policymakers decided to [keep rates at 3.75%](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/czx1vly05pvo). In the end, the Bank's monetary policy committee [voted unanimously to keep rates at 3.75%](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/c8d5dee3ep8o). [## Are UK interest rates expected to fall soon?](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/c3dky111m40o). That was the same as the prior month which had marked the slowest pace since May. In March, the US Federal Reserve [held its target interest rate](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/c36364d06x3o) at a range of 3.50% to 3.75% — its lowest level in three years. Earlier in the year, the Fed had [come under attack from US President Donald Trump](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/c4geyrdprwjo) for not cutting rates. The UK's rate of inflation has fallen. In the year to March 2025, the consumer price index (CPI) rose by 2.6% – 0.2 percentage points lower
